source:
On March 8, the U.S. Senate passed the Lend-
Lease Act. The support for the U.S. has
increased in England and Holland.
target: 3月8日，美國參議院通過了《租借法案》。英國與荷蘭對美國的支持度上升了。

source:
The United States will not fight in this war,
but we must serve as an armory for the nations
of democracy.
target: 美國不會參與這場戰爭，但我們必須成為民主國家的兵工廠。

source:
The German battleship, Bismarck, was sunk in
the Atlantic on May 27.
target: 5月27日，德國戰艦俾斯麥號在大西洋被擊沉。

source:
Superb! Our Royal Navy sunk Hitler's proud
Bismarck! The biggest threat in the Atlantic
has been destroyed.
target: 太棒了！皇家海軍擊沉了希特勒引以為傲的俾斯麥號！大西洋最大的威脅已被摧毀。

source:
Although we lost the Bismarck, we still have
our sea wolves - the U-boats. Paralyze the
British sea transport and starve them.
target: 雖然失去了俾斯麥號，我們還有海狼——U型潛艇。癱瘓英國的海上運輸，讓他們挨餓吧。

source:
On June 22, the Third Reich breached the non-
aggression pact with Moscow and launched the
Barbarossa Operation to embark on the invasion
of the USSR.
target: 6月22日，第三帝國撕毀了與莫斯科的互不侵犯條約，發動巴巴羅薩行動，開始入侵蘇聯。

source:
I declare the launch of the Barbarossa
Operation! With blitzkrieg by our Panzer,
Moscow will surrender before winter.
target: 我宣布巴巴羅薩行動開始！憑藉裝甲部隊的閃電戰，莫斯科將在冬天來臨前投降。

source:
My fellow citizens! We must stand up and
fight for our mother country! We must not let
invaders take a single grain of wheat or a drop
of gasoline!
target: 同胞們！我們必須奮起為祖國而戰！絕不能讓侵略者奪走一粒小麥、一滴汽油！

source:
On July 28, the Japanese army began occupying
Southern Indochina.
target: 7月28日，日軍開始進駐法屬印度支那南部。

source:
The German-controlled French government has
accepted our demand to occupy Indochina. This
will secure us a foothold to the south.
target: 德國控制下的法國政府已接受我方進駐印度支那的要求。這將確保我們南進的立足點。

source:
In response on the Japanese aggression in
French Indochina, the U.S. government froze all
Japanese assets in the U.S. The Japanese product-
ivity has dropped by 10%.
target: 為回應日本在法屬印度支那的侵略行動，美國政府凍結了日本在美國的全部資產。日本的生產力下降了10%。

source:
The Japanese are disturbing peace in Asia by
imposing a selfish rationale. To stop their
aggression, we will have to take sanctions
against them.
target: 日本以自私的論調擾亂亞洲和平。為了制止其侵略，我們不得不對其實施制裁。

source:
The U.S. imposed an oil embargo against Japan.
The Japanese productivity has dropped by 10%.
target: 美國對日本實施石油禁運。日本的生產力下降了10%。

source:
As long as Japan maintains its armed diplo-
macy, we won't be able to continue the peace
conferences.
target: 只要日本繼續推行武力外交，和平會談就無法繼續下去。

source:
Our fuel supply will last for only one more
year. We must quickly decide whether we'll fall
with or without fighting.
target: 我們的燃料只夠再撐一年。是戰是亡，必須儘快做出抉擇。

source:
The Japanese government has concluded that war
is inevitable unless there is a breakthrough in
the negotiations with the U.S. by early October.
target: 日本政府認定，除非十月上旬前與美國的談判出現突破，否則戰爭在所難免。

source:
Mainland China is the lifeline of our army.
It's absolutely impossible to accept America's
demand for our complete withdrawal. I'm afraid
war is inevitable.
target: 中國大陸是我軍的生命線，絕不可能接受美國全面撤軍的要求。恐怕戰爭已不可避免。

source:
The negotiation with Japan is coming to a
close. With our economic blockade pressing them
hard, it's a matter of time that they'll give in
to us.
target: 與日本的談判即將落幕。在我方經濟封鎖的重壓下，他們屈服只是時間問題。

source:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a
report on the statements from foreign leaders.
target: 外務省發表了各國領袖聲明的報告。

source:
We welcome Japan's diplomatic efforts. Depend-
ing on the circumstances, we may approve Japan's
interest in the Pacific.
target: 我們歡迎日本的外交努力。視情況而定，我們可能承認日本在太平洋的利益。

source:
Our country has no intention to intervene
in the political and military affairs in the
Pacific. We will maintain neutrality.
target: 我國無意干涉太平洋的政治與軍事事務，我們將保持中立。

source:
The United States is our comrade and the only
major power capable of overturning Fascism.
target: 美國是我們的同志，也是唯一有能力推翻法西斯主義的大國。

source:
I welcome Japan's diplomatic efforts. If we
can regain our land from the Germans, we will
not hesitate to support Japan.
target: 我歡迎日本的外交努力。若能從德國人手中收復失地，我們將不遺餘力支持日本。

source:
We will stand neutral regarding the conflicts
in the Pacific. We have no intention to make
political and military interventions.
target: 對於太平洋的衝突，我們將保持中立，無意進行政治與軍事干涉。

source:
The United States is a symbol of freedom. Only
the United States will be able to restore the
Dutch land now occupied by the Germans.
target: 美國是自由的象徵。只有美國能光復如今被德國占領的荷蘭國土。

source:
It's time to end the conflict with the
Japanese. If they support our rights and
interests, why not work with them for the
development of Asia!
target: 是時候結束與日本的衝突了。若他們支持我方的權益，何不攜手共謀亞洲的發展！

source:
We condemn the aggression of Imperial Japan.
If the United States are to stop them, we will
give our support.
target: 我們譴責日本帝國的侵略。若美國出面制止，我們將給予支持。

source:
Amid the fight against the Germans, we do not
wish to provoke the Japanese and their army in
Manchuria. We value friendly relations with
Japan.
target: 在與德國交戰之際，我們不願刺激日本及其駐滿洲的軍隊。我們重視與日本的友好關係。

source:
Amid the fight against the Germans, we can't
afford to spread our forces to the Far East. We
will stand neutral for the time being.
target: 在與德國交戰之際，我們無力將兵力分散到遠東，暫時將保持中立。

source:
We are struggling against the Germans. We ask
the U.S. to open the second front and press
Germany.
target: 我們正與德軍苦戰。請求美國開闢第二戰場，對德國施壓。

source:
Japan is an important friend as well as a
former ally in the Tripartite Pact. I urge Japan
to declare war against the British and the
Russians as soon as possible.
target: 日本是重要的朋友，也是三國同盟的前盟友。我敦促日本儘快對英國與蘇聯宣戰。

source:
The goal of Germany is the unification of
Europe. War in the Pacific is not our concern.
target: 德國的目標是統一歐洲。太平洋的戰爭與我們無關。

source:
It is not in our best interest to face the
U.S. military power in Europe. Depending on the
circumstances, we may choose to ally with the
U.S.
target: 在歐洲面對美國的軍事力量不符合我們的最大利益。視情況而定，我們可能選擇與美國結盟。

source:
The State Department issued a report on the
statements from foreign leaders.
target: 國務院發表了各國領袖聲明的報告。

source: The fleet is in a dock.

We've run into a storm and the
crew is busy dealing with it.
target: 我們遭遇了風暴，乘員正忙於應對。

source:
We're running low on material.
Request permission to supply
the fleet.
target: 物資即將耗盡。請求准許為艦隊進行補給。

source:
A typhoon is developing
nearby. We're paying careful
consideration to our course.
target: 附近有颱風正在發展。我們正謹慎考量航線。

source:
Heavy fog, low visibility. All
aircraft have been grounded.
target: 濃霧，能見度低。所有飛機均已停飛。

source:
The fleet is badly damaged.
Urgently request permission
to dry dock.
target: 艦隊受損嚴重。緊急請求准許入塢修理。

source:
Few aircraft assigned to
carriers. Request permission
to supply aircraft.
target: 航空母艦搭載的飛機不足。請求准許補充飛機。

source:
An enemy fleet may be nearby.
Request permission to search
the area with scouts.
target: 附近可能有敵方艦隊。請求准許以偵察機搜索該區域。

source:
Sailing in enemy waters.
Request permission to send
out escorts.
target: 正在敵方海域航行。請求准許派出護衛艦。

source:
An enemy submarine is nearby.
We will maintain our alert.
target: 附近有敵方潛艇。我們將保持警戒。

source:
Torpedo bombers on board
fleet are not armed. Request
permission to rearm aircraft.
target: 艦隊搭載的魚雷轟炸機尚未武裝。請求准許重新武裝飛機。

source:
Approaching %-10s
and waiting for battle orders.
target:
正在接近 %-10s
並等待作戰命令。

source:
Ready and waiting for battle
orders.
target: 準備就緒，等待作戰命令。

source:
This fleet is too weak.
Request permission to re-
organize.
target: 這支艦隊戰力太弱。請求准許重新編組。

source:
This fleet is too weak.
We will try to avoid any
skirmishes.
target: 這支艦隊戰力太弱。我們將盡量避免交戰。

source:
All is well and we're sailing
on course.
target: 一切正常，正沿航線前進。

source:
Fleet is well. Ready to
attack upon your orders.
target: 艦隊狀況良好，隨時可依您的命令發動攻擊。

source:
Ready to attack. Waiting for
further orders.
target: 攻擊準備就緒。等待進一步命令。

source:
Fleet is docked at port and
unable to attack.
target: 艦隊停泊在港口，無法發動攻擊。

source:
Enemy is sure to attack.
Request back up at once.
target: 敵人勢必來攻。請立即增援。

source:
We are encountering strong
winds. Our aircraft cannot
take off.
target: 我們正遭遇強風，飛機無法起飛。

source:
Heavy fog, low visibility.
Our aircraft cannot take off.
target: 濃霧，能見度低。飛機無法起飛。

source:
Land forces are engaged in
battle. Request permission to
assist.
target: 地面部隊正在交戰。請求准許支援。

source:
We have few materials
remaining. Restricting A.F.
action to a minimum.
target: 剩餘物資不多，將航空隊行動限制在最低限度。

source:
Few aircraft assigned.
Request permission to supply
aircraft.
target: 配屬的飛機不足。請求准許補充飛機。

source:
An enemy fleet may be nearby.
Request permission to search
the area.
target: 附近可能有敵方艦隊。請求准許搜索該區域。

source:
Our air force is too weak. We
should avoid all skirmishes.
target: 我方航空隊戰力太弱，應避免一切交戰。

source:
Our air forces suffer from
inexperience.

Aircraft not equipped with
torpedoes. Air force not
suitable for the strike.
target: 飛機未裝備魚雷，航空隊不適合執行此次攻擊。

source:
Request permission to bomb an
enemy base.
target: 請求准許轟炸敵方基地。

source:
I have nothing to report,
sir.
target: 長官，沒有需要報告的事項。

source:
A large enemy force is
expected to attack. Request
back up immediately.
target: 預計敵方大軍將發動攻擊。請立即增援。

source:
The unit is badly damaged. We
need a replacement unit at
once.
target: 部隊受損嚴重。亟需替補部隊即刻到達。

source:
The unit is badly damaged.
Request permission to disen-
gage from battle.
target: 部隊受損嚴重。請求准許脫離戰鬥。

source:
The unit is badly damaged.
Request immediate supplies.
target: 部隊受損嚴重。請求立即補給。

source:
The unit is badly damaged.
Battles will be avoided.
target: 部隊受損嚴重，將避免戰鬥。

source:
There is no need to defend
this area. Request permission
to be sent to the front.
target: 此區域無需防守。請求准許調往前線。

source:
We've encountered an enemy
unit. Request permission to
engage in battle.
target: 遭遇敵方部隊。請求准許交戰。

source:
Estimation of the Budget Expenditures 

       New Warship   :  1000/ship           Base Upkeep :   500/base
       Warship Repair:   250/ship           New Harbor  :  3000/each
       New Aircraft  :    10/plane          New Airfield:  2000/each
       Division      :    10/unit           Technology  :   500/topic
       Foreign Expen.:  1000/nation         Government  :  minimum 5000
target:
預算支出估算

       新建軍艦  ： 1000/艘           基地維持費：  500/基地
       軍艦修理  ：  250/艘           新建港口  ： 3000/座
       新建飛機  ：   10/架           新建機場  ： 2000/座
       師團      ：   10/支           科技      ：  500/項
       外交支出  ： 1000/國           政府支出  ： 最低5000

source:
Estimation of the Material Expenditures

       Fleet Usage   :  3000/fleet          A.F. Usage  :   250/base
       New Warship   :  2500/ship           Base Upkeep :   500/base
       Warship Repair:   250/ship           New Harbor  :  5000/each
       New Aircraft  :    20/plane          New Airfield:  3000/each
       Division      :   150/unit           National    :  minimum 10000
target:
物資支出估算

       艦隊使用  ： 3000/艦隊         航空隊使用：  250/基地
       新建軍艦  ： 2500/艘           基地維持費：  500/基地
       軍艦修理  ：  250/艘           新建港口  ： 5000/座
       新建飛機  ：   20/架           新建機場  ： 3000/座
       師團      ：  150/支           國家支出  ： 最低10000

source:
Drafting Forces

 Navy  : Assign up to 20 reserve units to the Marines.

 Army  : Assign up to 30 units to the army reserves.

 GNP   : You may draft 1 unit for every 1 point of GNP when your
         industry is 50 or higher.

         You may draft 1 unit for every 2 points of GNP when your
         industry is 49 or lower.

         The maximum number of people you may draft is equal to your
         GNP minus 20.

 Europe: The number of units sent to fight in the European front.
target:
徵用兵力

 海軍：最多可將20支預備隊配屬給陸戰隊。

 陸軍：最多可將30支部隊配屬給陸軍預備隊。

 GNP ：工業值在50以上時，每1點GNP可徵用1支部隊。

         工業值在49以下時，每2點GNP可徵用1支部隊。

         可徵用的最大人數等於GNP減20。

 歐洲：派往歐洲戰線作戰的部隊數。

source:
Technology

 Spy Tech.  : The technology of information gathering. An increase in spy
              technology improves the capacity of electronic weapons and
              the success rate of searching for enemies.

 Naval Tech.: The technology of developing advanced warships. An increase in
              naval technology shortens the time to repair or build ships.

 Aeronautics: The technology of developing advanced aircraft. It affects the
              rate of determining successful hits in an air battle.

 Weaponry   : The technology of building new weapons. It affects the rate of
              determining successful hits in battles.

 Industry   : The technology of producing industrial outputs. It affects the
              costs to build and repair weapons.
target:
科技

 間諜科技：情報蒐集的技術。提升間諜科技可增強
              電子兵器的性能，並提高搜索敵人的
              成功率。

 海軍科技：開發先進軍艦的技術。提升海軍科技
              可縮短修理或建造艦船的時間。

 航空技術：開發先進飛機的技術。影響空戰中
              命中判定的成功率。

 武器技術：建造新武器的技術。影響戰鬥中
              命中判定的成功率。

 工業　　：生產工業產出的技術。影響建造與
              修理武器的成本。

source:
Base Transports: Ships used to transport materials from
                  the bases to the mainland on the first
                  day of each month. Up to 200 units of
                  materials may be loaded per transport.
target:
基地運輸船：每月第一天將物資從各基地
                  運回本土的船隻。每艘運輸船
                  最多可裝載200單位物資。

source:
Base Materials: Each base needs materials for fleet and
                 land force supplies, warship repairs,
                 and base air force mobility points.
target:
基地物資：每個基地都需要物資，用於艦隊
                 與地面部隊的補給、軍艦修理，
                 以及基地航空隊的行動點數。

source:
I will explain the current
status of our country.The first chart shows our
diplomatic relations with
major countries.The situation is not good.We should build up our
forces that surround Japan.The next chart shows the
state of our country.If you wish to utilize a
radar system, we need more
investment in spy technology.Our productivity is slow-
ing down. We must urgently
secure a sufficient national
budget.We should improve our
technology in all areas and
overpower the Japanese.It's safe to say that we
have sufficient resources.Next is the status of our
Navy. Please look at this
chart.We need more fleet bombers
and land attack aircraft in
reserve. Please speed up their
production.We need more Navy scouts
in reserve. Please speed up
their production.We need more marines in
reserve. We should draft more
men and increase the reserve.With our reserve forces
running low, it's difficult to
continue the war. We need to
find a solution immediately.We need more aircraft in
reserve. Please speed up their
production.We need more marines in
reserve. Please draft more men
and increase the reserve.We have sufficient reserve
forces at this time. The
Japanese forces will be easy
to defeat.Next, I'll show you our
sphere of influence. Please
look at the map of the
Pacific.A surprise attack on Hawaii
is the main task this time. We
don't need to secure a supply
route.Occupation of Manila in the
Philippines is essential to
secure resources in South
Asia.Seizing Guadalcanal will
enable us to cut off communi-
cations between the U.S. and
Australia.If the enemy seizes Saipan
or Guam, our Absolute Defense
Parameter will collapse from
the Central Pacific.We should occupy Midway and
use it as a foothold to attack
Hawaii.The fall of Manila will
sever our supply route from
the south. That will cause a
serious shortage of supplies.The fall of Iwo Jima and
Naha will enable the
Americans to invade mainland
Japan.Defense of Manila is vital
to block Japan's advance to
the south.We should launch a counter-
offensive starting with the
recapture of the Gilbert and
Marshall naval bases.The capture of Saipan and
Guam will enable us to isolate
the Truk Island, the largest
enemy post in the Pacific.Midway is an outpost for
our home port in Hawaii. We
must defend it at all costs.Guadalcanal provides a vital
supply route between the U.S.
and Australia.Capturing Manila is the best
way to sever Japan's supply
route from the south.We can completely isolate
mainland Japan by gaining
control of Iwo Jima and
Naha.Next I will explain the
distribution of our forces.
Please look at the map.Our main objective is to
launch a surprise attack on
Hawaii. Land forces will not
be required.We should concentrate our
forces in the Philippines,
Malay, and Dutch East Indies.We should reinforce New
Guinea and Burma to block
counterattacks from the U.S./
British forces.I think we should concentrate
our forces in the Central
Pacific around Saipan and
Guam.If we are to sever the U.S.
and Australia, we should
immediately reinforce the New
Guinea front.We are severly outnumbered
by the enemy. It will be diff-
icult to regain the advantage.We should fortify our
defense in Iwo Jima and
Naha to delay the enemy's
invasion into mainland Japan.We should concentrate our
forces in the Philippines and
fortify our defense in the
south.Australia could be used as a
base to deploy forces to New
Guinea.We should concentrate our
forces in the Central Pacific
and New Guinea and take the
offensive from the two areas.We should concentrate our
forces in Saipan and Guam to
take the Philippines.If we concentrate all our
forces to capture Iwo Jima and
Naha, victory will be ours.Lastly, this is the location
of our fleets in the Pacific.This is the end of the
report. I'll wait for your
instructions, Commander.Judging from the circum-
stances, I believe the final
goal for this operation should
be one of these three.Please choose one.We will put this operation in
action according to this goal.
We promise you a sweeping
victory, Commander!1. Occupy Hong Kong, Manila, Singapore, Batavia, and Balikpapan by 2/15/42.2. Destroy the British Royal Navy by 2/15/42.3. Establish supply routes from Japan to Batavia by 2/15/42.1. Sink 10 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by 2/15/42.2. Sink 30 or more warships by 2/15/42.3. Hold Singapore and occupy Saigon, Hanoi, and Bangkok by 2/15/42.1. Occupy Midway and Attu Island by June 18, 1942.2. Sink 3 or more aircraft carriers by June 18, 1942.3. Sink an aircraft carrier and occupy Midway by June 18, 1942.1. Sink 4 or more aircraft carriers, or 6 or more battleships by 6/18/1942.2. Sink 20 or more warships by 6/18/1942.3. Hold the Midway Base and occupy Wake by 6/18/1942.1. Occupy at least two bases in Guadalcanal, E. Santo, or Fiji by 9/28/42.2. Occupy Port Moresby by 9/28/42.3. Hold the Truk Base and sink 30 or more warships by 9/28/42.1. Sink 8 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by 9/28/42.2. Hold the P. Moresby Base and sink 30 or more enemy warships by 9/28/42.3. Occupy at least two bases in Truk, Rabaul, or Wewak by 9/28/42.1. Sink 10 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by 6/9/44.2. Hold the Saipan and Guam bases, and sink 30 or more warships by 6/9/44.3. Hold Saipan and occupy the Marshall and Gilbert Bases by 6/9/44.1. Occupy three bases in Saipan, Guam, Palau, or Biak by July 9, 1944.2. Sink 3 or more aircraft carriers and occupy Saipan by July 9, 1944.3. Sink 10 or more carriers or battleships by July 9, 1944.1. Sink 10 or more aircraft carriers or battleships by Nov. 23, 1944.2. Hold the Manila Base and sink 30 or more warships by Nov. 23, 1944.3. Occupy Saipan and Guam by Nov. 23, 1944.1. Occupy Manila, Davao, and Palau by 11/23/44.2. Sink 10 or more carriers or battleships by 11/23/44.3. Sink 20 or more warships including 1 dreadnought battleship by 11/23/44.1. Sink 8 or more aircraft carriers, or 10 or more battleships by 4/11/45.2. Sink 30 or more warships by 4/11/45.3. Establish supply routes between Japan and Truk by 4/11/45.1. Occupy Iwo Jima and Naha by 4/11/1945.2. Sink the dreadnought battleship, Yamato, and occupy Naha by 4/11/1945.3. Annihilate the Japanese fleet by 4/11/1945.
target:
接下來說明我國
目前的狀況。第一張圖表顯示我國
與主要國家的
外交關係。情勢並不樂觀。我們應強化
包圍日本的兵力。下一張圖表顯示
我國的狀態。若要運用雷達系統，
需要增加對間諜科技
的投資。我國的生產力正在
下滑。我們必須緊急
確保充足的國家預算。我們應提升各領域的
科技，壓倒日本人。可以說我們的
資源相當充足。接下來是海軍的狀況。
請看這張圖表。我們需要更多艦載轟炸機
與陸上攻擊機的預備機。
請加快生產。我們需要更多海軍
偵察機的預備機。
請加快生產。我們需要更多陸戰隊
預備隊。應徵召更多人員
以擴充預備隊。預備兵力即將枯竭，
難以繼續作戰。
必須立即找出對策。我們需要更多飛機的
預備機。請加快生產。我們需要更多陸戰隊
預備隊。請徵召更多人員
以擴充預備隊。目前預備兵力充足。
要擊敗日本軍隊
將輕而易舉。接下來展示我國的
勢力範圍。請看
太平洋地圖。奇襲夏威夷是本次的
主要任務，無需確保
補給線。占領菲律賓的馬尼拉，
是確保南亞資源
不可或缺的行動。奪取瓜達康納爾，
便能切斷美國與
澳洲之間的交通。若敵人奪取塞班或關島，
我國的絕對國防圈將
從中太平洋崩潰。我們應占領中途島，
作為進攻夏威夷的
立足點。馬尼拉陷落將切斷
來自南方的補給線，
導致物資嚴重短缺。硫磺島與那霸陷落，
將使美國人得以
入侵日本本土。守住馬尼拉是阻擋
日本南進的關鍵。我們應從奪回吉爾伯特
與馬紹爾的海軍基地開始，
發動反攻。攻占塞班與關島，
便能孤立太平洋上最大的
敵方據點特魯克島。中途島是夏威夷母港的
前哨，必須不惜一切
代價守住。瓜達康納爾是美國與
澳洲之間的重要
補給通道。攻占馬尼拉是切斷日本
南方補給線的
最佳手段。只要控制硫磺島與那霸，
便能完全孤立
日本本土。接下來說明我方的
兵力部署。請看地圖。主要目標是奇襲夏威夷，
無需動用地面部隊。應將兵力集中於菲律賓、
馬來與荷屬東印度。應增援新幾內亞與緬甸，
阻擋美英軍隊的反擊。我認為應將兵力集中於
中太平洋的塞班與
關島一帶。若要切斷美國與澳洲，
應立即增援
新幾內亞戰線。我方兵力遠遜於敵，
要挽回優勢
將十分困難。應強化硫磺島與那霸的
防衛，延緩敵人
入侵日本本土。應將兵力集中於菲律賓，
鞏固南方的防衛。可以澳洲為基地，
向新幾內亞派遣兵力。應將兵力集中於中太平洋
與新幾內亞，自兩地
展開攻勢。應將兵力集中於塞班
與關島，以奪取菲律賓。若集中全力攻占硫磺島
與那霸，勝利將屬於我們。最後，這是我方艦隊
在太平洋的位置。報告完畢。恭候您的
指示，司令。從情勢判斷，本次作戰的
最終目標應是
以下三者之一。請選擇其一。我們將依此目標執行作戰。
司令，我們保證為您
帶來全面的勝利！1. 於2/15/42前占領香港、馬尼拉、新加坡、巴達維亞與巴厘巴板。2. 於2/15/42前殲滅英國皇家海軍。3. 於2/15/42前建立日本至巴達維亞的補給線。1. 於2/15/42前擊沉10艘以上航空母艦或戰艦。2. 於2/15/42前擊沉30艘以上軍艦。3. 於2/15/42前保有新加坡，並占領西貢、河內與曼谷。1. 於1942年6月18日前占領中途島與阿圖島。2. 於1942年6月18日前擊沉3艘以上航空母艦。3. 於1942年6月18日前擊沉1艘航空母艦並占領中途島。1. 於6/18/1942前擊沉4艘以上航空母艦，或6艘以上戰艦。2. 於6/18/1942前擊沉20艘以上軍艦。3. 於6/18/1942前保有中途島基地並占領威克島。1. 於9/28/42前占領瓜達康納爾、聖埃斯皮里圖或斐濟之中至少兩處基地。2. 於9/28/42前占領莫士比港。3. 於9/28/42前保有特魯克基地並擊沉30艘以上軍艦。1. 於9/28/42前擊沉8艘以上航空母艦或戰艦。2. 於9/28/42前保有莫士比港基地並擊沉30艘以上敵方軍艦。3. 於9/28/42前占領特魯克、拉包爾或韋瓦克之中至少兩處基地。1. 於6/9/44前擊沉10艘以上航空母艦或戰艦。2. 於6/9/44前保有塞班與關島基地，並擊沉30艘以上軍艦。3. 於6/9/44前保有塞班，並占領馬紹爾與吉爾伯特基地。1. 於1944年7月9日前占領塞班、關島、帛琉或比亞克之中的三處基地。2. 於1944年7月9日前擊沉3艘以上航空母艦並占領塞班。3. 於1944年7月9日前擊沉10艘以上航空母艦或戰艦。1. 於1944年11月23日前擊沉10艘以上航空母艦或戰艦。2. 於1944年11月23日前保有馬尼拉基地並擊沉30艘以上軍艦。3. 於1944年11月23日前占領塞班與關島。1. 於11/23/44前占領馬尼拉、達沃與帛琉。2. 於11/23/44前擊沉10艘以上航空母艦或戰艦。3. 於11/23/44前擊沉20艘以上軍艦，其中包含1艘無畏級戰艦。1. 於4/11/45前擊沉8艘以上航空母艦，或10艘以上戰艦。2. 於4/11/45前擊沉30艘以上軍艦。3. 於4/11/45前建立日本與特魯克之間的補給線。1. 於4/11/1945前占領硫磺島與那霸。2. 於4/11/1945前擊沉無畏級戰艦大和號，並占領那霸。3. 於4/11/1945前殲滅日本艦隊。

The task force is advancing
smoothly and is currently
sailing 230 miles north of the
Oahu Island of Hawaii.Unless we destroy the U.S.
fleet now, they will surely be
a threat to our Navy in the
future.The fate of our Empire lies in
this battle. I want everyone
to be prepared.Today, the task force led by
Vice Admiral Chuichi Nagumo
cast off and headed for Pearl
Harbor.Other fleets at port will
soon set sail for their res-
pective missions.This mission must succeed in
order for us to gain the
advantage in this war.The fate of our Empire lies in
this battle. I want everyone
to be prepared.Since we were unable to
destroy the enemy's carriers,
we have left our country open
to air raids.In the battle at the Coral
Sea, we were unable to destroy
the enemy's carriers.This time we must lure the
enemy fleet into battle and
surround them with our super-
ior naval forces.The situation is tense. The
U.S. is counterattacking in
several areas across the
Pacific.We should expect strong
resistance from the U.S. now.We cannot afford a threat to
the boundaries of our national
defense.The surprise attack on Pearl
Harbor was a success, but this
war has just begun.Our victory in this war is
linked to securing the south-
ern islands that are rich in
natural resources.By occupying Singapore and
Manila, we can advance into
the Dutch East Indies.Since we were unable to
destroy the enemy's carriers,
we have left our country open
to air raids.In the battle at the Coral
Sea, we were unable to destroy
the enemy carriers.This time we must lure the
enemy fleet into battle and
surround them with our
superior naval forces.U.S. forces are starting to
land troops on the island of
Guadalcanal.The enemy is on the offensive
now.Our forces are suffering
from the four carriers we lost
in the battle at Midway.To cut off the supply routes
between the U.S. and Australia,
however, we cannot give up
Guadalcanal to the Americans.I am the new Commander of
the Combined Fleet, Soemu
Toyoda.The enemy's strength has
intensified and they have a
secure foundation to take over
the Pacific.In their next move, they
will probably try to occupy
the Mariana Islands, Saipan,
and Guam.Saipan and Guam are essential
to our national defense. We
must defend them with our
lives!The U.S. is trying to cut off
our supplies by dispatching
large forces to the Philip-
pines.We will be in a desprate
situation if our supply routes
are severed.This is a decisive battle
for our survival in this war.
Attack with all your might!A massive fleet with over
one hundred enemy ships has
surrounded Iwo Jima.By losing several warships
in the battles near the
Philippines,our fleet's ability to
accomplish our mission is
close to zero.But we mustn't give up just
yet! Let's show the U.S. the
spirit of a Japanese soldier!Due to bad weather conditions, the aircraft
carrying Commander Koga and others moving the
Combined Fleet Command Station to Davao was lost.Remains of the aircraft were never found.Following Admiral Isoroku Yamamoto, the
Japanese had lost their second commander to
the war.It seemed like an omen of the future for the
Japanese Imperial Navy.U.S.-Japan negotiations have
broken down.Be prepared for hostile
actions from the Japanese
within a matter of days.The Japanese movements
suggest military and naval
operations near the Philip-
pines and Malay.However, we can't seem to
locate their carrier forces.We must keep an eye out for
them.It looks like the enemy is
planning for an attack on
Midway. I'm afraid we only have 3
carriers to dispatch against
their massive task force.We'll have to count on our
crew to pull us through and
defeat the Japanese.Operations are proceeding as
planned.Yesterday's raid against the
Japanese at Truk was a great
success.I doubt we'll be seeing any
Japanese fleets in the mid-
Pacific any time soon.Continue to advance towards
the Japanese mainland!Yesterday, the Japanese bombed
Pearl Harbor and delivered a
deadly blow to our naval
forces.It's my fault that we didn't
see this coming.The next Japanese naval
target will probably be the
Philippines.We cannot give them another
Pearl Harbor. We'll wait for
our chance and counterattack
with vigor!It looks like the enemy is
planning for an attack on
Midway.I'm afraid we only have 3
carriers to dispatch against
their massive task force.We'll have to count on our
crew to pull us through and
defeat the Japanese.It wouldn't be an exaggeration
if I said the battle at Midway
has claimed revenge for Pearl
Harbor.We've succeeded in occupying
the Japanese airfield on
Guadalcanal Island.If the Japanese win back
this airfield, our lines of
communication with Australia
will be severed.We must avoid such an act at
all costs.The B-29 long-range bomber
has finally been completed.The aircraft is worthy of its
name, the "Flying Fortress".The bomber's attacking radius
indicates that an air strike
launched from Saipan orGuam would be ideal to attack
the Japanese mainland.We need to secure these
bases, if we are to wage
successful air strikes on
Japan!This is the time to rescue
the Philippines from the hands
of the Japanese!With the success of this
mission we can sever the
supply routes to Japan.The Japanese forces will be
driven to self-destruction
without sources of oil.Liberate the Philippines,
and raise the American flag in
Manila!It will be too difficult to
attack Japan from the Mariana
Islands.We need to secure bases that
are close to the mainland. Iwo
Jima and Naha are optimal
locations.We can expect heavy resist-
ance, but if we succeed in
occupying these bases,we can reduce the entire
country of Japan to ashes.Occupying Iwo Jima and Naha
could ensure our victory in
this war!
target:
任務部隊進展順利，
目前正航行在夏威夷
歐胡島以北230英里處。除非現在殲滅美國艦隊，
否則他們日後必將成為
我國海軍的威脅。帝國的命運繫於此一戰。
全員務必做好覺悟。今天，南雲忠一中將
率領的機動部隊拔錨起航，
駛向珍珠港。停泊在港的其他艦隊
也即將為各自的
任務啟航。此次任務必須成功，
我們才能在這場戰爭中
取得優勢。帝國的命運繫於此一戰。
全員務必做好覺悟。由於未能摧毀敵方
航空母艦，我國本土已
暴露在空襲威脅之下。在珊瑚海海戰中，
我們也未能摧毀敵方的
航空母艦。這次必須誘敵艦隊出戰，
以我方優勢的海軍兵力
將其包圍殲滅。局勢緊張。美國正在
太平洋多個區域
展開反攻。應預料到美國
此時的頑強抵抗。國防圈邊界
不容受到威脅。對珍珠港的奇襲成功了，
但這場戰爭才剛開始。此戰的勝利關係到能否
確保資源豐富的
南方諸島。占領新加坡與馬尼拉後，
我們便可進軍
荷屬東印度。由於未能摧毀敵方
航空母艦，我國本土已
暴露在空襲威脅之下。在珊瑚海海戰中，
我們也未能摧毀敵方
航空母艦。這次必須誘敵艦隊出戰，
以我方優勢的海軍兵力
將其包圍殲滅。美軍正開始在
瓜達康納爾島登陸。敵人現已轉入攻勢。我方因在中途島海戰中
損失的四艘航空母艦
而元氣大傷。然而為了切斷美國與
澳洲之間的補給線，
我們絕不能將瓜達康納爾
拱手讓給美國人。我是聯合艦隊新任司令
豐田副武。敵人的實力不斷增強，
已具備席捲太平洋的
穩固基礎。他們下一步很可能
企圖占領馬里亞納群島、
塞班與關島。塞班與關島是我國
國防的命脈，
我們必須誓死捍衛！美國正派遣大軍前往
菲律賓，企圖切斷
我們的補給。若補給線被切斷，
我們將陷入絕境。這是關乎此戰存亡的
決戰。全力出擊！一支擁有超過百艘敵艦的
龐大艦隊已包圍硫磺島。由於在菲律賓近海的戰鬥中
損失了多艘軍艦，
我方艦隊達成任務的能力
幾乎為零。但我們還不能放棄！
讓美國人見識
日本軍人的精神！由於天候惡劣，搭載古賀司令等人前往達沃、
轉移聯合艦隊司令部的飛機失事了。飛機殘骸始終未被尋獲。繼山本五十六大將之後，
日本又失去了第二位司令。這彷彿是日本帝國海軍
未來的不祥之兆。美日談判已經破裂。數日之內務必防備
日本的敵對行動。日軍的動向顯示其可能在
菲律賓與馬來附近
展開陸海軍行動。然而，我們尚未掌握
其航空母艦部隊的位置。必須嚴密監視他們。看來敵人正計劃
進攻中途島。恐怕我們只有
3艘航空母艦可派遣，
對抗其龐大的機動部隊。我們只能依靠全體乘員
帶領我們度過難關，
擊敗日本。作戰正按計畫進行。昨日對特魯克日軍的
空襲大獲全勝。短期內，中太平洋
應該不會再出現
日本艦隊了。繼續向日本本土挺進！昨天，日本轟炸了珍珠港，
給我國海軍帶來
致命一擊。未能預見此事
是我的失職。日本海軍的下一個目標
可能是菲律賓。我們絕不能讓
珍珠港事件重演。
等待時機，奮起反擊！看來敵人正計劃
進攻中途島。恐怕我們只有3艘
航空母艦可派遣，
對抗其龐大的機動部隊。我們只能依靠全體乘員
帶領我們度過難關，
擊敗日本。若說中途島海戰已為
珍珠港報了一箭之仇，
一點也不誇張。我們已成功占領
瓜達康納爾島上的
日軍機場。若日本人奪回這座機場，
我們與澳洲之間的
交通線將被切斷。必須不惜一切代價
避免此事發生。B-29長程轟炸機
終於完成了。這架飛機不愧其
「空中堡壘」之名。以其攻擊半徑來看，從塞班
或關島發動空襲是攻擊
日本本土的理想選擇。若要對日本實施
成功的空襲，我們必須
拿下這些基地！現在正是從日本人手中
解救菲律賓的時刻！此次任務成功，我們便能
切斷日本的補給線。失去石油來源，
日本軍隊將走向
自我毀滅。解放菲律賓，
在馬尼拉升起美國國旗！從馬里亞納群島攻擊日本
太過困難。我們需要確保靠近其本土的
基地。硫磺島與那霸
是最佳位置。預料會遭遇頑強抵抗，
但若成功占領這些基地，我們便能將整個日本
化為焦土。占領硫磺島與那霸，
將確保我們贏得
這場戰爭！
